task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screaming Frog SEO Spider application window is visible on the screen.

The URL 'https://crawler-test.com/' is entered in the URL bar at the top of the application, and the crawl has started or completed (indicated by a progress bar or a populated list of URLs in the main pane).
The 'Directives' tab is selected in the main interface. Optionally, a filter for 'X-Robots-Tag' may be applied, or columns showing X-Robots-Tag data are visible.
An 'Export' or 'Save As' dialog box is open, initiated from the Directives tab.

task_completion.description: Save the exported data to the correct file and directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the filename 'x_robots_header_report.csv' and the directory path indicates it is being saved in 'Documents/SEO/exports' (or a file manager shows the file successfully saved there).

integrity.description: The agent used the Screaming Frog GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The crawl and export actions are performed within the Screaming Frog SEO Spider graphical interface, not via terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The results appear to be from a genuine crawl
integrity.visual_evidence: The URLs and data in the Screaming Frog window are populated automatically by the software and belong to crawler-test.com, rather than being manually typed.

integrity.description: The CSV file was exported, not manually created
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV file is generated via the application's export feature, not by opening a text editor or spreadsheet program and manually typing the expected answers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://crawler-test.com/. Expected filename: x_robots_header_report.csv. Export directory: /home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports. The X-Robots-Tag header exists on the test pages and Screaming Frog detects and exports it via the Directives tab.
